decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity violated the upper bound (500.0). To improve heat spreading without violating bounds, I will jointly increase the spreader's geometric cross-section (width and height) to their maximum allowed single-step limits. This increases the effective thermal mass and lateral area for heat dissipation.

changes.reason: Increase spreader width by ~11% (within 1.5x limit) to maximize lateral heat spreading area towards the domain boundaries.
Increase spreader height by 50% (max allowed step) to increase vertical thermal cross-section and reduce resistance between chip and spreader.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the spreader layer
Lower peak chip temperature due to improved lateral distribution
Movement of chip_max_temperature closer to or below 85.0°C
risk_notes: Increasing spreader height reduces clearance to the top boundary; ensure no overlap with other potential components (none currently defined above).
Geometric changes may require mesh adaptation in subsequent steps if convergence issues arise, though current mesh density should suffice.
